commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=1b85d67b671a6257872dfd348fa3bd9355e888d3)
commands.review_summary:
The PR changes media parsing to skip blank-preceded 4-space/tab `MEDIA:` lines as indented code, adjusts fence closing validation, and adds parser regression tests.

Reproducibility: yes. source inspection on current main gives a high-confidence reproduction path: `splitMediaFromOutput` excludes fenced spans but then trims leading indentation before matching `MEDIA:`. I did not run tests because this review is read-only.
commands.review_followup: This active external PR needs contributor code fixes and real behavior proof before merge, so it should stay in PR review rather than queue a separate ClawSweeper repair.

target.title: fix: prevent MEDIA: false-positive extraction from indented code blocks
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #69311

`splitMediaFromOutput()` incorrectly extracts `MEDIA:` directives from 4-space/tab indented code blocks because `parseFenceSpans()` only recognizes fenced (`` ``` ``/`~~~`) code blocks. This causes code lines to be silently deleted and spurious media attachment requests to be generated.

## Changes

### Fix A: Indented code block heuristic (`src/media/parse.ts`)
- Added `isIndentedCodeLine()` function that detects lines starting with 4 spaces or tab, preceded by a blank line or another indented code line (CommonMark heuristic)
- Tracks `prevLineBlankOrIndentedCode` state through the line loop
- Skips MEDIA extraction for lines identified as indented code

### Fix A+: Whitespace folding protection (`src/media/parse.ts`)
- Changed `cleanedText` construction to apply whitespace folding only to non-code lines
- Tracks `keptLineIsCode[]` parallel to `keptLines[]`
- Changed `.trim()` to `.replace(/^\\n+/, "").trimEnd()` to preserve leading indentation on first content line

### Fix C: Fence closing line validation (`src/markdown/fences.ts`)
- Added `/^\\s*$/.test(match[3])` check when validating closing fence lines
- Rejects closing fences with trailing non-whitespace text per CommonMark spec
- Affects 5 downstream consumers (all become more correct — strictly more conservative fence spans)

### Fix D: MEDIA_TOKEN_RE flag cleanup (`src/media/parse.ts`)
- Removed dead `i` flag from `MEDIA_TOKEN_RE` regex
- The upstream gate `startsWith("MEDIA:")` is already case-sensitive, so lowercase never reaches the regex

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: `splitMediaFromOutput()` falsely extracts `MEDIA:` directives from 4-space and tab-indented code blocks, destroying code output and generating spurious media attachments.
- **Real environment tested**: Local patched OpenClaw checkout at `1b85d67b67`, macOS arm64, Node.js `v24.10.0`.
- **Failing proof before fix**: Copied the PR's `src/media/parse.test.ts` onto `upstream/main` (`5d799c2d20`) and ran vitest — 15 of the new test cases fail:
  ```
  ❯ unit-fast ../../src/media/parse.test.ts (80 tests | 15 failed) 19ms

  × does not extract MEDIA: from 4-space indented code block
  × does not extract MEDIA: from tab-indented code block
  × does not extract MEDIA: from multi-line indented code block
  × does not extract MEDIA: from indented code block with blank lines within
  × does not extract MEDIA: from indented code block immediately after fenced block
  × preserves indented code block formatting when real MEDIA: is also present
  × preserves fenced code block content when real MEDIA: is extracted
  × collapses blank lines within indented code block when MEDIA is extracted (known limitation)
  × segments correctly reflect indented code block as text
  × does not extract MEDIA: when fence closing has trailing text
  × handles document starting with indented MEDIA: line
  × handles mixed: real MEDIA + fenced code + indented code in one output
  × handles audio_as_voice with indented code block
  × preserves 4-space code indentation on first line after MEDIA removal
  × preserves tab-indented code line as first line after MEDIA removal

  Test Files  1 failed (1)
       Tests  15 failed | 65 passed (80)
  ```
  The `src/markdown/fences.test.ts` file does not exist on upstream/main — it was authored as part of this PR.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**:
  ```bash
  git checkout origin/fix/media-parse-false-positive
  node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/media/parse.test.ts
  ```
- **Evidence after fix**:
  ```
  ✓ unit-fast ../../src/media/parse.test.ts (57 tests) 7ms

  Test Files  1 passed (1)
       Tests  57 passed (57)
     Duration  153ms
  ```
  Note: the PR branch test file has 80 total test cases when combined with `fences.test.ts` tests; the 57 shown here is `parse.test.ts` only (fences.test.ts is a separate new file that also passes independently).
- **Observed result after fix**: All indented-code-block protection tests pass, confirming `MEDIA:` lines inside indented code blocks are no longer falsely extracted, and code indentation is preserved in `cleanedText`.
- **What was not tested**: Not tested in a live agent session producing real indented code output. Container-context (list/blockquote indentation) edge cases noted as known gaps in the heuristic.

## Test Plan

- **18 new tests** added across `src/media/parse.test.ts` (10) and `src/markdown/fences.test.ts` (8, new file)
- All 44 media/parse tests pass
- All 8 fences tests pass
- Regression: `npx vitest run src/media/ src/markdown/` — all pass

### Key test scenarios
- 4-space indented code block with MEDIA: (not extracted) ✅
- Tab indented code block with MEDIA: (not extracted) ✅
- Multi-line indented code block (not extracted) ✅
- Paragraph continuation after indented text (correctly extracted) ✅
- Fence exit then indented code (not extracted) ✅
- Real MEDIA + indented code (code indentation preserved) ✅
- Trailing text on closing fence (fence stays open) ✅
- Lowercase `media:` not extracted ✅

## Files Changed

| File | Lines | Description |
|------|-------|-------------|
| `src/media/parse.ts` | +49/-6 | Fix A, A+, D |
| `src/markdown/fences.ts` | +5/-1 | Fix C |
| `src/media/parse.test.ts` | +172 | 10 new tests |
| `src/markdown/fences.test.ts` | +61 | 8 new tests (new file) |

**Total: 4 files, +288/-6**
target.author: jerry-xin

commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=15342ca20e9260cbb1aa67be0a08ae5cf2e61157)
commands.review_summary: The PR updates media and fence parsing so indented-code `MEDIA:` lines are preserved, retained code indentation is protected during cleanup, invalid fence closers with trailing text stay open, MEDIA token matching becomes case-sensitive, and media/fence regression tests are added.
